attempts.raw:
Based only on the supplied papers and passages, here is the structured assessment. The critical observation is that none of the ten retrieved papers actually study HMGN3 itself; they concern related-but-distinct proteins (PTPN22, HMGN1, HMGN2, HMGB2, PCSK9) or generic TCR topics.

```json
{
  "tf": "HMGN3",
  "query": "HMGN3 TCR inhibition",
  "papers_retrieved": 10,
  "papers_relevant": 0,
  "paper_assessments": [
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC11237943",
      "retrieval_rank": 1,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human primary T cells; TCR signalling",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "PTPN22 is a known inhibitor of T cell receptor (TCR) signaling.",
      "rationale": "Directly addresses TCR-signalling inhibition but via PTPN22, not HMGN3; provides no evidence about the candidate regulator."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC4126642",
      "retrieval_rank": 2,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human primary CD8+ T cells and PBMCs",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "Activated CD8+ T cells release HMGN2, which directly kills tumor cells.",
      "rationale": "Studies HMGN2 (a different family member) as a secreted cytotoxic effector; not HMGN3 and not TCR inhibition."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC12346228",
      "retrieval_rank": 3,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "review",
      "experimental_system": "Human; TCR repertoire / adoptive cell therapy",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "Explores using individual patient TCR repertoire data to select T cells for immunotherapy.",
      "rationale": "Review of TCR repertoire-guided therapeutic TCR selection; no mention of HMGN3 or any inhibitory phenotype."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC6352494",
      "retrieval_rank": 4,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Non-human (mouse) tumor-infiltrating CD8+ T cells",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "Combination treatment reversed T cell exhaustion, leading to robust anti-tumor effects.",
      "rationale": "Concerns the alarmin HMGN1 reversing exhaustion, not HMGN3; no TCR-signalling readout and wrong molecule."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC11014665",
      "retrieval_rank": 5,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human/mouse xenograft; CD4 TCR-engineered T cells",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "CD4 TCR-engineered T cells targeting EBNA1 significantly inhibited nasopharyngeal cancer growth.",
      "rationale": "Adoptive TCR-T therapy against EBV antigen; no HMGN3 content and no inhibitory TCR phenotype."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC10499904",
      "retrieval_rank": 6,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Non-human (mouse) exhausted CD8+ T cells; chronic infection/cancer",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "HMGB2 expression is critical for exhausted CD8+ T cell differentiation.",
      "rationale": "Studies HMGB2 in exhaustion differentiation, not HMGN3; no impaired-TCR-function readout for the candidate."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC12261488",
      "retrieval_rank": 7,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "computational or observational",
      "experimental_system": "Computational; TCR sequence generation/binding prediction",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "A diffusion-based model generates epitope-specific TCRs and predicts TCR-epitope binding.",
      "rationale": "Deep-learning method for TCR design; no HMGN3 and no inhibitory T cell biology."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC12047442",
      "retrieval_rank": 8,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human/mouse; CD8+ T cells in hepatocellular carcinoma",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "Targeting HMGB2 with tannic acid reversed T cell exhaustion and inhibited tumor growth.",
      "rationale": "Concerns HMGB2 in exhaustion/metabolism, not HMGN3; provides no evidence about the candidate regulator."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC11302870",
      "retrieval_rank": 9,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",

      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "Inhibiting PCSK9 enhances effectiveness of TCR-T cells and anti-PD-1 immunotherapy.",
      "rationale": "Studies PCSK9-mediated metabolic enhancement of T cell function, not HMGN3; no inhibitory-phenotype link to candidate."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC8704919",
      "retrieval_rank": 10,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Non-human (mouse) tumor-specific T cells",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "High TCR signal strength leads to T cell dysfunction, while low strength causes inertness.",
      "rationale": "Examines TCR signal-strength thresholds and dysfunction generally; makes no reference to HMGN3."
    }
  ],
  "overall_evidence_tier": "none",
  "key_paperclip_result_ids": [],
  "tier_rationale": "No retrieved paper studies HMGN3. The results concern distinct molecules (PTPN22, HMGN1, HMGN2, HMGB2, PCSK9) or generic TCR/immunotherapy topics. None provide primary experimental, computational, or associative evidence materially linking HMGN3 to TCR-signalling inhibition or a closely related inhibitory T cell phenotype, so no tier above none can be supported.",
  "confidence": "high"
}
```

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human primary T cells; TCR signalling
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: PTPN22 is a known inhibitor of T cell receptor (TCR) signaling.
paper_assessments.rationale: Directly addresses TCR-signalling inhibition but via PTPN22, not HMGN3; provides no evidence about the candidate regulator.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human primary CD8+ T cells and PBMCs
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Activated CD8+ T cells release HMGN2, which directly kills tumor cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: Studies HMGN2 (a different family member) as a secreted cytotoxic effector; not HMGN3 and not TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human; TCR repertoire / adoptive cell therapy
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Explores using individual patient TCR repertoire data to select T cells for immunotherapy.
paper_assessments.rationale: Review of TCR repertoire-guided therapeutic TCR selection; no mention of HMGN3 or any inhibitory phenotype.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Combination treatment reversed T cell exhaustion, leading to robust anti-tumor effects.
paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns the alarmin HMGN1 reversing exhaustion, not HMGN3; no TCR-signalling readout and wrong molecule.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human/mouse xenograft; CD4 TCR-engineered T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: CD4 TCR-engineered T cells targeting EBNA1 significantly inhibited nasopharyngeal cancer growth.
paper_assessments.rationale: Adoptive TCR-T therapy against EBV antigen; no HMGN3 content and no inhibitory TCR phenotype.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human (mouse) exhausted CD8+ T cells; chronic infection/cancer
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: HMGB2 expression is critical for exhausted CD8+ T cell differentiation.
paper_assessments.rationale: Studies HMGB2 in exhaustion differentiation, not HMGN3; no impaired-TCR-function readout for the candidate.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: A diffusion-based model generates epitope-specific TCRs and predicts TCR-epitope binding.
paper_assessments.rationale: Deep-learning method for TCR design; no HMGN3 and no inhibitory T cell biology.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human/mouse; CD8+ T cells in hepatocellular carcinoma
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Targeting HMGB2 with tannic acid reversed T cell exhaustion and inhibited tumor growth.
paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns HMGB2 in exhaustion/metabolism, not HMGN3; provides no evidence about the candidate regulator.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human/mouse; CD8+ TCR-T cells in hepatocellular carcinoma
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Inhibiting PCSK9 enhances effectiveness of TCR-T cells and anti-PD-1 immunotherapy.
paper_assessments.rationale: Studies PCSK9-mediated metabolic enhancement of T cell function, not HMGN3; no inhibitory-phenotype link to candidate.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: High TCR signal strength leads to T cell dysfunction, while low strength causes inertness.
paper_assessments.rationale: Examines TCR signal-strength thresholds and dysfunction generally; makes no reference to HMGN3.

parsed.tier_rationale: No retrieved paper studies HMGN3. The results concern distinct molecules (PTPN22, HMGN1, HMGN2, HMGB2, PCSK9) or generic TCR/immunotherapy topics. None provide primary experimental, computational, or associative evidence materially linking HMGN3 to TCR-signalling inhibition or a closely related inhibitory T cell phenotype, so no tier above none can be supported.
